expectations.text: Warning callout uses correct syntax: > [!warning] with the blocker message
expectations.evidence: Line 1-2: '> [!warning]' followed by '> **Blocker:** API rate limits may delay the Q2 release — needs escalation by EOW'. Correct Obsidian callout syntax used.

expectations.text: Tip callout uses collapsed syntax: > [!tip]- (with minus for collapsed by default)
expectations.evidence: Line 4: '> [!tip]- How to check current rate limit status'. The minus sign is present, making it collapsed by default.

expectations.text: Callout content matches the requested text accurately
expectations.evidence: Warning content includes 'API rate limits may delay the Q2 release — needs escalation by EOW'. Tip content includes 'curl https://api.internal/status' and 'rate_limit_remaining' field reference — both match requested content.

expectations.text: Does not use incorrect callout delimiters (no triple backtick code blocks instead of > syntax)
expectations.evidence: Both callouts use '>' prefix syntax correctly. Triple backticks appear only inside the tip callout to format the curl command (lines 6-8), not as a replacement for callout delimiters — this is acceptable and standard Obsidian practice.

expectations.text: Provides the content in a form ready to paste or append to the file (e.g., shows the exact markdown)
expectations.evidence: The output file contains only the two callout blocks with no surrounding prose or explanation — pure markdown ready to paste directly into an Obsidian note.

suggestions: Consider whether triple backtick code blocks inside callouts are preferred over inline code for the curl command — both are valid, but consistency with vault style may matter.
The timing.json does not include a separate executor_duration_seconds field; future runs could track agent execution time separately from total wall-clock time.
eval_feedback.overall: Perfect score — all 5 expectations passed. The agent produced clean, correctly-formatted Obsidian callouts with the right syntax for both warning and collapsed tip types, accurate content, and output ready to paste. No errors encountered.
